goal: At 2am, only show what's realistically open and deliverable right now.

screens.name: Night Mode Toggle
screens.purpose: Entry point to switch the whole app into the filtered emergency view.

states.success: Simple switch/banner; auto-suggested (not forced) after a certain local hour.

screens.name: Night Catalog
screens.purpose: Filtered vendor list — only open-now, sorted by distance, defaulted to emergency categories.

states.empty: No open vendors within default radius -> explicit 'Nothing open nearby' + auto-expanded radius, not a dead end.
states.success: List sorted by distance, each row shows ETA + open-until time, category chips (Pharmacy/Baby/Essentials) pinned at top.
states.error: Location permission denied -> manual location entry fallback.

screens.name: Vendor Detail (Night)
screens.purpose: Stripped-down vendor page — just what's needed for a fast emergency order.

states.success: Product list limited to emergency-relevant categories, ETA prominent, checkout streamlined.

userFlow: It's past midnight; app suggests Night Mode via a banner.
User enables it -> night_catalog immediately filters to open pharmacies/stores nearby.
Selects a vendor, sees only emergency-relevant categories.
edgeCases: Nothing open within default radius -> auto-expand and say so explicitly, don't just show an empty screen.
A vendor marked is24Hour but is actually closed for a holiday -> out of scope for v1 (no holiday-hours data), acceptable known limitation.
Location denied -> manual address entry, never a hard stop.
notes: Lowest technical risk in the whole feature set — a query filter and a UI state, no external dependency. Good first build for an early demoable win.
